item.title: JAX backends and devices 摘要
item.description:
The author describes porting PyTorch LLM code to JAX and encountering a CUDA out-of-memory error when loading a 19GiB dataset via Safetensors, because JAX loads data directly to GPU by default. They explore JAX's device management, discovering that `jax.devices()` only shows devices for the default backend (GPU if available). They find that `jax.default_device` can be used as a context manager to temporarily set the default device to CPU, allowing large datasets to be loaded into RAM instead of VRAM. The post explains the difference between JAX backends and devices, and contrasts with PyTorch's explicit device management.
标签: JAX, CUDA, GPU, memory management, Safetensors, LLM, PyTorch, device management, XLA, deep learning

item.title: Elon Musk’s X Is a Freak Show 摘要
item.description:
Nate Silver analyzed Twitter/X engagement data from 2026, finding the platform dominated by low-quality, highly partisan right-wing accounts, with Elon Musk receiving an algorithmic boost. Silver argues the platform is rigged, not merely abandoned by left-leaning users. The top accounts are obscure slop accounts, while Musk's own account is the most engaged. The author concludes that competing on X is futile unless content aligns with Musk's preferences, though interesting people still post there.
标签: Twitter, X, Elon Musk, social media, algorithm, engagement, right-wing, Nate Silver, platform manipulation, quality

item.title: Nieman Journalism Lab: Twitter/X Punishes Accounts That Post Links 摘要
item.description:
Laura Hazard Owen used Claude to scrape 200 recent tweets from 18 large publishers' X accounts, analyzing engagement (likes, comments, retweets). Six publishers have paywalls (Bloomberg, CNN, Forbes, NYT, WSJ, WaPo), nine don't (Al Jazeera, AP, BBC, Breitbart, CBS, Daily Wire, Fox, NBC, Reuters), plus three engagement-maxing accounts. Results show links in tweets significantly reduce engagement; traditional link-heavy publishers cluster in low-engagement zones regardless of follower count. The analysis suggests X penalizes link posts, reinforcing its walled-garden nature.
标签: Twitter, X, engagement, news publishers, paywalls, social media, journalism, AI scraping, Claude, links

item.title: Pluralistic: Refining humanity (05 Jun 2026) 摘要
item.description:
Cory Doctorow explores how programming computers forces us to confront the complexity and ambiguity of seemingly simple concepts, like defining the letter 'A' or alphabetizing movie titles. He argues that computers, lacking human context and flexibility, expose the tacit assumptions in our knowledge and social systems. This process of turning tacit knowledge into explicit instructions reveals broken assumptions and clarifies what we are as humans. Doctorow connects this to the rise of spectrum-based identities and the need for systemic change when digital systems fail to accommodate human diversity.
标签: technology, philosophy, programming, cognition, society, complexity, identity, digital systems, tacit knowledge, human-computer interaction

item.title: Why all the PRs? 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses how AI-generated PRs are being used to game hiring signals. Historically, developers built websites or contributed to open source to demonstrate skills. Now, candidates use AI to inflate GitHub contributions, creating hollow PRs that burden maintainers without genuine investment. The author argues this misaligns intentions: contributors seek resume padding, while projects need problem-solving and collective memory. This trend devalues authentic signals and adds noise to hiring.
标签: AI, open source, hiring, GitHub, PRs, resume padding, signal, developer culture, job market, slop
